title: Provider Support & Call Intelligence
description:
Call center analytics for a national health information network / clearinghouse that handles millions of provider support calls per year. When providers need help with prior authorizations, claims status, eligibility verification, benefit inquiries, or enrollment, they call into outsourced partner agencies.

Every call is transcribed via ASR, scored against a quality rubric (35% transcription confidence + 35% section coverage + 20% PII redaction compliance + 10% disposition quality), and analyzed for sentiment using AI-generated summaries. Supervisors use this data to coach agents, track compliance, and identify systemic issues before they become provider complaints or SLA breaches.

This room covers call quality scoring with agent rankings and rolling trends, AI-generated sentiment analysis (overall tone, opening/closing sentiment, trajectory arc), and compliance tracking with week-over-week comparison and consecutive-day streak detection. Built for call center supervisors, QA analysts, and provider relations teams.

question: Average handle time by agency
Top-ranked agents by agency
Call types with most negative sentiment
How does AHT vary by call type?
Which agents need coaching?
Which agencies are missing FCR target?
Sentiment distribution across all calls
Average speed to answer trend last 30 days
Agents trending downward on rolling average
Top emotional markers in high-risk calls
Which agencies are below compliance threshold?
Declining sentiment calls with complaints
What is our First Call Resolution rate this week?
Average call quality score by agency
Compliance trend last 30 days

content:
## Who uses this room
Call center supervisors, QA analysts, provider relations leads, and operations managers at a national health information network's provider support floor. Provider calls come into multiple outsourced agencies for prior authorizations, claims status, eligibility, benefit inquiries, or enrollment.

## Two distinct surfaces in this room
- **QA scoring** — genie_call_scores, genie_call_sentiment, genie_compliance_daily, mv_call_quality_metrics. Quality, sentiment, compliance streaks.
- **Call operations** — genie_call_ops_daily, mv_call_ops_metrics. Timings: FCR, AHT, ASA, queue, abandonment.
Pick the right surface for the question. QA scoring = how well the call went. Operations = how fast / efficiently.

## Domain dictionary
- Call quality scoring rubric: 35% transcription confidence + 35% section coverage + 20% PII redaction compliance + 10% disposition quality. Score 0-100.
- score_band: Excellent (>=90) / Good (75-89) / Needs Coaching (60-74) / At Risk (<60).
- Compliance threshold: 0.85 (85%). Below-compliance day = compliance_rate < 0.85.
- FCR = First Call Resolution = resolved without a callback within 7 days. Healthcare benchmark: aim for >=70%.
- AHT = Average Handle Time (seconds). Healthcare benchmark: ~720s (12 min) target.
- ASA = Average Speed to Answer = wait_seconds. Healthcare benchmark: ~264s (4.4 min) target.
- Abandoned proxy: wait_seconds > 600 (10 min). Industry abandonment alarm threshold: > 8%.
- sentiment_trajectory: stable / improving / declining / volatile / stable_high / stable_low / declining_then_flat.

## SOURCE PREFERENCE (critical for accuracy)
- For call quality aggregates (compliance rate, average score, high risk pct, escalation rate) — prefer MEASURE(<measure_name>) on mv_call_quality_metrics.
- For call operations aggregates (FCR pct, AHT, ASA, call volume) — prefer MEASURE(<measure_name>) on mv_call_ops_metrics.
- Do NOT recompute these from base views unless the user asks for row-level detail.
- MEASURE() aliases are NOT visible in HAVING. Always wrap MEASURE() in a CTE and filter / order in the outer SELECT.

## Guardrails
- Never expose actual call transcripts — only AI-generated summaries and quality scores are available.
- Compliance rate questions default to last 30 days; FCR / AHT / ASA default to last 14 days.
- 'Declining' sentiment trajectory with a normal score band is an early warning — flag it.
- For 'top agents' questions, require minimum call volume (>=20 calls) to avoid single-call outliers.

## Beyond this room
Document processing, OCR quality, Fellegi-Sunter match outcomes, PA decisions/SLAs, payer mix: 'Clinical Document Processing Intelligence' room.

question: High risk calls with declining sentiment
sql: SELECT c.call_id, c.agent_name, c.call_score, c.disposition, s.sentiment_overall, s.sentiment_trajectory, s.summary_text FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_scores c JOIN serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_sentiment s ON c.call_id = s.document_id WHERE c.outcome_bucket = 'high_risk' AND s.sentiment_trajectory = 'declining' ORDER BY c.call_score
usage_guidance: Use for identifying worst-case calls combining low scores with declining sentiment

question: Agencies with compliance streaks above 3 days
sql: SELECT agency_name, call_type, day, compliance_rate, days_below_threshold FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_compliance_daily WHERE days_below_threshold > 3 ORDER BY days_below_threshold DESC, day DESC
usage_guidance: Use for compliance streak alerts or systemic agency issues

question: Agencies below the 70% FCR healthcare benchmark
sql: WITH m AS (SELECT agency_name, MEASURE(fcr_pct) AS fcr FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.mv_call_ops_metrics GROUP BY agency_name) SELECT agency_name, fcr FROM m WHERE fcr < 70 ORDER BY fcr ASC
usage_guidance: Use this when the user asks about FCR misses, struggling agencies, or coaching priorities.

question: AHT by call type trend last 14 days
sql: WITH m AS (SELECT ops_date, call_type, MEASURE(avg_handle_seconds) AS aht_sec FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.mv_call_ops_metrics WHERE ops_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 14 DAYS GROUP BY ops_date, call_type) SELECT * FROM m ORDER BY ops_date DESC, aht_sec DESC
usage_guidance: Use for call duration trends, AHT analysis, or handle-time-by-type questions.

question: FCR, AHT, ASA per agency using the metric view
sql: WITH m AS (SELECT agency_name, MEASURE(fcr_pct) AS fcr, MEASURE(avg_handle_seconds) AS aht_sec, MEASURE(avg_wait_seconds) AS asa_sec FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.mv_call_ops_metrics GROUP BY agency_name) SELECT * FROM m ORDER BY fcr DESC
usage_guidance: Use mv_call_ops_metrics MEASURE() for the governed FCR / AHT / ASA numbers. Always wrap in a CTE before ordering.

question: Bottom 10 agents by average score with call counts
sql: SELECT agent_name, agency_name, ROUND(AVG(call_score), 1) AS avg_score, COUNT(*) AS calls FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_scores GROUP BY agent_name, agency_name ORDER BY avg_score LIMIT 10
usage_guidance: Use for worst performing agents, coaching candidates, or bottom agent rankings

question: Sentiment distribution for escalated calls
sql: SELECT s.sentiment_overall, s.sentiment_trajectory, COUNT(*) AS call_count FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_sentiment s JOIN serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_scores c ON s.document_id = c.call_id WHERE c.disposition = 'escalated' GROUP BY s.sentiment_overall, s.sentiment_trajectory ORDER BY call_count DESC
usage_guidance: Use for sentiment analysis on escalated or problematic calls

question: Agency compliance ranking with rolling trend
sql: SELECT agency_name, day, compliance_rate, rolling_7d_compliance, agency_compliance_rank FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_compliance_daily WHERE day >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL 7 DAYS ORDER BY day DESC, agency_compliance_rank
usage_guidance: Use for agency comparison, compliance leaderboard, or ranking questions

instruction: Use when user asks about persistent compliance problems or systemic issues (> 3 consecutive days below threshold).
Use when user asks about high risk, problematic, or failing calls.
Use when user asks about escalations or supervisor transfers.
Use when user asks about calls that went badly or declining sentiment.
Use as time dimension for daily trending of call scores.
Group by agent for agent-level analysis.
Group by sentiment: positive, neutral, negative, mixed.
Group by call type: prior_auth_inquiry, claims_status, eligibility_check, benefit_inquiry, provider_enrollment.
Group by partner agency for agency comparison.
Use for escalations, supervisor transfers, or escalated call counts.
Use for average quality score, mean score, or overall call quality.
Use for high risk calls, failed calls, or calls below 70.

sql: ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_scores.`outcome_bucket` = 'compliant' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*), 2)
instruction: Use for compliance rate, compliance percentage, or what fraction are compliant.
Use for longest streak, worst compliance streak, or consecutive days below 85%.

question: Bottom 10 agents by average score with call counts

content: WITH agg AS (SELECT agent_name, agency_name, AVG(call_score) AS avg_score, COUNT(*) AS n_calls FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_scores GROUP BY agent_name, agency_name HAVING COUNT(*) >= 20) SELECT agent_name, agency_name, ROUND(avg_score, 1) AS avg_score, n_calls FROM agg ORDER BY avg_score ASC LIMIT 10

question: Calls with declining sentiment trajectory in the last week

content: SELECT call_type, sentiment_start, sentiment_end, COUNT(*) AS n FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_sentiment WHERE sentiment_trajectory = 'declining' GROUP BY call_type, sentiment_start, sentiment_end ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 20

question: Sentiment distribution for escalated calls

content: SELECT sentiment_overall, COUNT(*) AS n FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_sentiment WHERE call_type ILIKE '%escal%' GROUP BY sentiment_overall ORDER BY n DESC

question: Agencies missing the 70% FCR benchmark

content: WITH m AS (SELECT agency_name, MEASURE(fcr_pct) AS fcr FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.mv_call_ops_metrics GROUP BY agency_name) SELECT agency_name, fcr FROM m WHERE fcr < 70 ORDER BY fcr ASC

question: FCR / AHT / ASA per agency

content: WITH m AS (SELECT agency_name, MEASURE(fcr_pct) AS fcr, MEASURE(avg_handle_seconds) AS aht_sec, MEASURE(avg_wait_seconds) AS asa_sec FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.mv_call_ops_metrics GROUP BY agency_name) SELECT * FROM m ORDER BY fcr DESC

question: Consecutive-day compliance streaks above 3 days

content: SELECT agency_name, call_type, day, days_below_threshold FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_compliance_daily WHERE days_below_threshold >= 3 ORDER BY days_below_threshold DESC, day DESC

question: Agencies below compliance threshold this month

content: SELECT agency_name, ROUND(AVG(compliance_rate)*100, 2) AS avg_compliance_pct FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_compliance_daily WHERE day >= DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', CURRENT_DATE) GROUP BY agency_name HAVING AVG(compliance_rate) < 0.85 ORDER BY avg_compliance_pct ASC

question: Top 10 agents by call quality score

content: WITH ranked AS (SELECT agent_name, agent_avg_score, agent_call_count, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY agent_avg_score DESC) AS rk FROM serverless_stable_swv01_catalog.genie_availity_ops.genie_call_scores WHERE agent_call_count >= 20) SELECT agent_name, agent_avg_score, agent_call_count FROM ranked WHERE rk <= 10 ORDER BY rk
